ksa_text: Knowledge of semantic layer and metrics layer design, including measure definition, dimension hierarchy design, slowly changing dimension handling, metric definition standards, and centralized metric consistency across reporting tools.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Data Architecture & Analytics KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.4) to justify inclusion for the GenAI CoE Leader. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world GenAI CoE Leader roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Recent job postings for GenAI CoE Leader roles increasingly include Data Architecture & Analytics competencies as organizations broaden role scope. External evidence supports inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Overturn: evolving market expectations for GenAI CoE Leader now include Data Architecture & Analytics competencies. Revised to include.

decision_rationale: Initially excluded but overturned through adversarial review. Data Architecture & Analytics knowledge KSA is relevant to the GenAI CoE Leader based on external evidence and internal consistency re-evaluation.
